text: Consider the React component below. Which statements accurately describe the state updates?

options.A: Clicking the button will always increment count by 2.
options.B: The `count` value inside the `handleClick` closure is fixed at the render time.
options.C: Using `setCount(prevCount => prevCount + 1)` would ensure increment by 2.
options.D: The component will re-render only once after `handleClick` completes.

text: Which Tailwind CSS utility classes would you use to create a button with a blue background, white text, padding, and rounded corners?

options.A: bg-blue-500 text-white p-4 rounded-lg
options.C: bg-blue-500 color-white p-4 rounded
options.D: blue-bg white-text p-4 rounded-corners

text: Given the Node.js code, what will be the output order in the console?

options.A: A, D, C, B
options.C: A, D, B, C
options.D: The order of B and C is non-deterministic.

text: Which statement best describes a key difference between MongoDB and traditional relational databases?

options.A: MongoDB uses SQL for querying, while relational databases use NoSQL.
options.B: MongoDB stores data in flexible JSON-like documents, relational databases use rigid tables.
options.C: MongoDB requires a predefined schema for all collections, relational databases are schemaless.
options.D: MongoDB supports ACID transactions, relational databases do not.

text: A React component fetches data when `userId` changes. What's the correct way to ensure the fetch re-runs only when `userId` changes?

options.A: Leave the dependency array empty (`[]`) to run once.
options.C: Omit the dependency array to run on every render.
options.D: Include `data` in the dependency array (`[userId, data]`).

text: You have an Express.js application with several middleware functions. In what order should they generally be placed?

options.A: Error handling -> Logging -> Authentication -> Route handlers
options.B: Logging -> Authentication -> Route handlers -> Error handling
options.C: Authentication -> Error handling -> Logging -> Route handlers
options.D: Route handlers -> Logging -> Authentication -> Error handling

text: Consider the Node.js code. If `doHeavyComputation()` is CPU-bound, which statements are true regarding its impact?

options.A: Requests to `/compute` will block other incoming requests.
options.B: The server will crash due to an unhandled promise rejection.
options.C: Requests to `/` will also be delayed while `/compute` is running.
options.D: Using `worker_threads` could mitigate the blocking issue.

text: You need to find all products from a MongoDB collection `products` that are in stock (quantity > 0) and cost less than $50. Which queries are correct?

options.A: db.products.find({ quantity: { $gt: 0 }, price: { $lt: 50 } })
options.C: db.products.find({ quantity > 0, price < 50 })
options.D: db.products.find({ $or: [{ quantity: { $gt: 0 } }, { price: { $lt: 50 } }] })

text: What will be logged to the console when the `Parent` component renders for the first time?

options.A: Nothing, because `Child` is a functional component.
options.C: 'Child rendered' twice.
options.D: An error, because `Child` is not memoized.

text: You want to hide an element on small screens and display it as a flex container on medium screens and larger. Which Tailwind CSS classes achieve this?

options.A: hidden md:flex
options.C: sm:hidden md:flex
options.D: hidden lg:flex

text: An Express.js route needs to extract a user ID from the URL path. Which options correctly define the route and access the ID?

options.A: app.get('/users/:id', (req, res) => { const userId = req.params.id; });
options.C: app.get('/users/id', (req, res) => { const userId = req.params.id; });
options.D: app.get('/users/:userId', (req, res) => { const userId = req.params.userId; });

text: Consider the React component. Why might `handleSave` log an outdated `count` value if `count` changes between renders?

options.A: The `useEffect` hook creates a closure over the `count` from its initial render.
options.B: The `setInterval` callback is not re-created when `count` changes.
options.C: The component is not re-rendering when `count` updates.
options.D: Adding `count` to the `useEffect` dependency array would fix this.

text: An Express.js endpoint receives user input. Which practices are crucial for preventing common security vulnerabilities?

options.A: Sanitizing user input to prevent XSS attacks.
options.B: Hashing passwords before storing them in the database.
options.C: Using prepared statements or parameterized queries for database interactions.
options.D: Returning detailed error messages to the client for debugging.

text: A MongoDB collection `orders` has millions of documents. Queries frequently filter by `customerId` and sort by `orderDate`. What's the best indexing strategy?

options.A: Create a single index on `customerId`.
options.C: Create a compound index on `{ customerId: 1, orderDate: -1 }`.
options.D: Create two separate indexes: one on `customerId` and one on `orderDate`.

text: You want to conditionally render a `WelcomeMessage` component only if `isLoggedIn` is true. Which React patterns achieve this correctly?

options.A: { isLoggedIn && <WelcomeMessage /> }
options.C: { if (isLoggedIn) { return <WelcomeMessage />; } }
options.D: { isLoggedIn || <WelcomeMessage /> }

text: In an Express.js application, an async middleware throws an error. Which statements are true about handling this error correctly?

options.A: The error will automatically be caught by the error-handling middleware.
options.B: You must wrap `someAsyncTaskThatMightFail()` in a `try...catch` block and call `next(error)`.
options.C: Express 5.x automatically catches errors from async functions in routes/middleware.
options.D: Using a package like `express-async-errors` can simplify error propagation.

text: Given the Node.js modules, what will be the output when `main.js` is executed?

options.C: 1 undefined
options.D: An error will occur.

text: Which statements accurately describe the differences between `props` and `state` in React functional components?

options.A: `props` are immutable and passed from parent to child.
options.B: `state` is mutable and managed within the component itself.
options.C: Changes to `props` always trigger a re-render, but `state` changes do not.
options.D: A child component can directly modify its parent's `props`.

text: You are designing a MongoDB schema for a `Book` collection. Each book has an `author`. When is it generally better to embed the author's details directly within the `Book` document?

options.A: If author details are frequently accessed with the book and rarely change.
options.B: If author details are very large and complex.
options.C: If an author can write many books and their details are frequently updated.
options.D: If you need to query authors independently of books.

text: You want to add a custom `primary` color to your Tailwind CSS configuration. How would you correctly extend the default theme?

options.A: colors: { primary: '#FF0000' }
options.C: theme: { colors: { primary: '#FF0000' } }
options.D: extend: { theme: { colors: { primary: '#FF0000' } } }

text: A Node.js server needs to handle large file uploads efficiently. Which approach is generally preferred for performance and memory usage?

options.A: Reading the entire file into a buffer before processing.
options.B: Using streams to process the file in chunks.
options.C: Storing the file directly in memory as a string.
options.D: Using synchronous file I/O operations.

text: Given a React component that passes a callback to a child. When is using `useCallback` on the `handleClick` function beneficial for performance?

options.A: Always, as it prevents unnecessary re-renders of the parent component.
options.B: When `ChildComponent` is wrapped in `React.memo` and `handleClick` is passed as a prop.
options.C: Only if `handleClick` performs a very expensive computation.
options.D: Never, `useCallback` is only for memoizing values, not functions.

text: What is the primary difference between `res.send()` and `res.json()` in Express.js?

options.A: `res.send()` can send any type of response, `res.json()` only sends JSON objects.
options.C: `res.send()` is synchronous, `res.json()` is asynchronous.
options.D: There is no functional difference; they are aliases.

text: Which MongoDB operations would you use to add a new user and then update their `email` address?

options.A: db.users.insert({ name: 'Alice' }); db.users.update({ name: 'Alice' }, { $set: { email: 'alice@example.com' } });
options.C: db.users.create({ name: 'Charlie' }); db.users.modify({ name: 'Charlie' }, { email: 'charlie@example.com' });
options.D: db.users.add({ name: 'David' }); db.users.edit({ name: 'David' }, { email: 'david@example.com' });

text: You have a Node.js application that performs CPU-intensive image processing. How can you leverage multi-core CPUs to improve throughput without blocking the event loop?

options.A: Use the `cluster` module to spawn multiple Node.js processes.
options.B: Implement `worker_threads` to offload the image processing.
options.C: Increase the `max-old-space-size` flag in Node.js.
options.D: Ensure all image processing operations are synchronous.
